transcript.clean_text: bit of a paul hanging over la welter after primos roglic decided that he could not take the start of stage 17 after this incident in the run to samaras while battling it out as he was with mads pedersen but more than that trying to put as much space between himself and remco evanopol as possible unfortunately pretty much roglic was to crash and crash out of this race so all change in the top ten everyone moving up and in fact louie menkes moving up into tenth place looking secure in red what would that do to the battle plans of those around over the pole would stage winds become ever more important and gaps for other places the podium of course had to be fought for and jumbo visma had to rethink their battle plans they lined up at the beginning of the day knowing that this was likely to be a last chance for a breakaway in this race and the terrain certainly suggested it just one climb to deal with and that coming right at the very end in the run up to monastero de tenturia tough climb difficult one to master and set a rhythm on likewise the rest of the day very very lumpy well out of the back door there was a downhill run which garnered speeds of around 50 kilometers per hour for the first 45 minutes and finally we had a 13-man break that was extraordinarily strong including veteran rigoberto iran fred wright twice third and two times in fourth quentin pacio has had very good strong runs in this race also up front was jesus sorella winner of stage seven mark saleh winner of stage five and the peloton as you can see was in relaxed mood for much of the day that's person was on water carrying duties while up ahead the break was well almost mimicking combat as fred wright threw the bike in a mock fashion at the sprint well it all got very real as we headed to the end of the day lawson credit knowing that he had to go early being a time trialist and not necessarily a climber he didn't want to share the finale with those with better mountain legs well they gave lawson credit perhaps not enough respect he went very deep into the climb in fact he had a maximum margin of 26 seconds over the chases and on occasion we thought it might well just be enough well the brakes started to fracture and the rolls of the dice began mark seller winner of stage five he was pushing on kentucky looked like he was struggling but would re-gather himself for more battles brigaber took around bridged over to craddock in the company of stage seven winner jesus herrera of coffee dis and others managed to come up to join the battle for home clermont a man who has a never say die attitude as we witnessed in last year's welter was up there around him went jesus harada and this looked like the move of the day well they reckoned without the doggy determination of frigabo to iran had preserved just enough energy to bridge over to harada and in the wake kentin pasha was coming up as well meanwhile the gc men were engaged in their own skirmish if there was a hunt for gaps out there then ever nepal and enrique mouse were not going to allow them to be upon them out front iran drove for the line kentin pasha rounded jesus harada to finish second but a great result for the veteran colombian now a winner of stages in all of the grand tours it was a big moment for him and for ef education easy post well we still had to wait for the favorites well they did fracture shout out made her the portuguese pushed on demon aaronsmann went out of stage 15 he went backwards out the group of favorites i made up hit the line five minutes in arrears and behind him evan paul coming home with then mcmas while the rest of the favorites followed suit with her to iran had more or less halved his deficit overall and would vault into the top 10 overall having started the day and was 15 minutes down he now up to ninth stage win and a great place overall extraordinarily welcome both to him and his team who are hunting for points as you know around taking it ahead of bashir harada solaire was fourth head of elisande shabazan damaki jungles chesapeake and credit no change in the overall well at least the first two there may well be some alteration the deeper we get into this race madrid we haven't reached it just yet but avatar leads mass by two minutes and one around up to ninth nine minutes and thirty three down and so what is on offer in the run to alto de beau now well a lot of climbing at the end of the day that's for sure two category one tests the same mountain taken twice we can't wait this race is not over yet
